prompt: Watercolour painting — an ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A 12-year-old Indian girl in a simple cotton frock seated cross-legged on a wooden verandah, reading aloud from a Kannada weekly magazine to a 62-year-old grandmother in a traditional white-and-maroon sari with grey hair pulled back. A small brass oil lamp glows between them, casting warm amber light on both faces. Background: a mud-plastered wall and a glimpse of a tiled-roof courtyard, with night-shadows behind. Dark warm twilight, loose watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain, soft earth tones — terracotta, ochre, deep amber. Both figures looking down at the magazine page together. No text, no labels.
alt: A 12-year-old girl reading aloud from a magazine to an older woman by lamplight in a tiled-roof village house, north Karnataka at dusk

prompt: Watercolour painting — an ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5). A dusty rural bus stop in a 1960s north Karnataka village at golden hour. A worn green-and-cream bus is pulled up; a conductor is handing down bundled stacks of newspapers and magazines tied with jute string. A few villagers — a man in a dhoti, an elderly woman in a sari, two barefoot children — wait near the bus, looking at the papers with quiet excitement. Tiled-roof houses behind, a banyan tree to one side. Warm amber light, dust motes in the bus headlight beam, loose watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain, soft earth tones. No text, no labels.
alt: A bus arriving at a small north Karnataka village stop with newspaper bundles being unloaded; villagers waiting in the dust at golden hour

prompt: Watercolour painting — a close-up. A pair of weathered, wrinkled hands of an elderly Indian woman rest on the edge of an open Kannada-script magazine page. The hands are softly lit by warm oil-lamp light from the lower right. The face is partially visible at the top of the frame — only the line of the chin and lower cheek — with a faraway, longing expression. The magazine page shows printed Kannada text with a small illustration of an old woman from the story. Dark background, soft amber and brown tones, loose watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain. No labels, no text other than the indistinct printed page text.
alt: An elderly woman's wrinkled hands resting on the edge of an open Kannada magazine, her eyes faraway, lamp light on the page

prompt: Watercolour painting — an ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5). A small Indian girl, about 12, in a cotton frock, walks softly toward her grandmother who is sitting alone on the parapet of a flat-roof terrace at night. A full moon sits in the dark sky. The grandmother holds an open Kannada magazine in her lap, her face turned slightly away, lit by soft silver moonlight. Stars faintly visible. The girl's silhouette is in the foreground, the grandmother in the middle ground. Dark navy and silver palette, warm amber from a distant oil lamp in a window of the house. Loose watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain. No text.
alt: A 12-year-old girl approaching her grandmother sitting silently on an open terrace at night under a full moon, holding a Kannada magazine in her lap

prompt: Watercolour painting — a close-up. An elderly woman's wrinkled hand and forearm are bowed down to touch the bare foot of a young girl sitting on a low wooden stool. The image is cropped to show only hands, feet, the edge of a white sari, and the hem of the girl's frock. Brass puja lamp visible in the bokeh background. Warm amber light from the lamp. Dark earthen-brown background, loose watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain. Soft, dignified, no faces. No text.
alt: Close-up painterly view of an elderly woman's hand bowed to touch a young girl's foot — the gesture of charan sparsh, here inverted (grandmother to granddaughter)
